frameworks.name: flexible working-memory recoding
frameworks.one_line: remembered information is recoded into the format best suited to upcoming behavior

frameworks.name: fixed-locus sustained-activity model of working memory
frameworks.one_line: visual information held by sustained spiking in fixed parietal and prefrontal stores

frameworks.name: sensory recruitment hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: visual memories maintained in early sensory cortex for comparison to future inputs

frameworks.name: multivariate fMRI decoding
frameworks.one_line: pattern-based readout of remembered content across cortical regions

core_question: Does the cortical format of visuo-spatial working memory change with whether the upcoming motor response is known?
key_assumptions: memory content is decodable from multivariate fMRI patterns
behavioral goals can reconfigure which cortical areas store the memory
spatial position and planned action are dissociable codes
manipulations: whether the required behavioral response was known or unknown during the delay
decoded region (early visual, parietal, somatosensory, motor, premotor)
content type decoded (remembered spatial position vs planned motor action)
key_findings: less spatial-position information in early visual and parietal regions when response was known
planned motor-action representations emerged in S1, M1, and premotor cortex when response was known
the trade-off between sensory and motor codes tracked the task condition
interpretations: working-memory networks are strategically reconfigured by upcoming behavioral demands
remembered visuo-spatial content can be recoded into an action-oriented motor format
